title: Exec session name collision: generated names use id[2:10] (100s bucket) → idempotency dedupe misroutes
description: Found dogfooding two concurrent 'arcmux-cli exec --agent codex' dispatches with the same --owner: CreateSession with empty name generates name=fmt.Sprintf("%s-%s", agent, id[2:10]) — id[2:10] is the first 8 digits of the nanosecond timestamp, identical for creates within the same ~100s window. Same (name, owner) → findNonTerminalByNameOwner idempotent_hit returned worker A's session to worker B; B then queued WaitIdle behind A and would have delivered its prompt into A's session (wrong cwd/thread). Daemon log evidence 15:32:19 session.create.idempotent_hit. Workaround used: distinct --owner per dispatch.
acceptance_criteria: Generated session names are collision-free for rapid creates (use the full id suffix like the HTTP path does, e.g. agent+id[2:]); regression test: two CreateSession calls with empty name + same owner in the same instant yield two distinct sessions (created=true twice); consider arcmux-cli exec --name passthrough. make validate green.
close_reason: Fixed: generated session names now use the full id suffix (agent + id[2:], matching the HTTP path) so rapid same-agent creates can no longer collide into the idempotency dedupe. Regression test TestCreateSessionGeneratedNamesNeverCollide proves two same-owner empty-name creates yield two distinct sessions (created=true twice). make validate pass. CLI --name passthrough deferred (workaround no longer needed).

title: Protocol state dir: ~/data/arcmux/sessions → ~/data/mux (shared mux protocol, not app dir)
description: Boyan directive: the centralized session-state contract is a PROTOCOL (something-mux), not arcmux's private storage. Move subscriber-facing state to ~/data/mux/: sessions/<id>.json (+archived) and the hook-output JSONL (also fixes the non-durable /tmp/arcmux-hooks default). arcmux-private state (profiles registry, state.bolt, pulse) stays in ~/data/arcmux/. Includes coded legacy migration sweep + docs/tickets updated across repos.
acceptance_criteria: defaults: session_state_dir=~/data/mux/sessions, hook_output_dir=~/data/mux/hook-output; daemon startup migrates legacy ~/data/arcmux/sessions/*.json when on defaults (idempotent); make validate green; live daemon writes new path; docs/state-dir.md + llm-classes + skill updated; mission-control ticket re-pointed
close_reason: Shipped d16a076: defaults moved to ~/data/mux/{sessions,hook-output}; coded idempotent startup migration (tested, verified live — daemon log shows migrated docs and all profiles on the new dir); docs/state-dir.md reframed as the mux protocol contract with ~/data/arcmux explicitly excluded as app-private; validate.md + skill updated; mission-control ticket re-pointed.

title: LLM classes: unified codex/claude/grok with interactive tmux + headless exec
description: Boyan ask: arcmux should natively support (1) starting a tmux session (under an arcmux socket) running codex/claude/grok, with prompt delivery verified via hooks writing to the centralized state dir (~/data/arcmux) that any subscriber (arcmux, mission-control) can read; (2) headless one-shot executions (codex exec, claude -p, grok -p) — both modes managed by one LLM class per agent so adding/configuring an LLM is one place. Callers: arcmux-cli (human) or the arcmux skill in ~/agents/blin-agents/skills.
design: Class registry in internal/profile: type Class{Name, Interactive Profile, Exec Profile}; DefaultClasses()=[codex,claude,grok]; DefaultProfiles() derived (<name> + <name>_exec) so existing profile names stay stable. Grok interactive: 'grok --no-alt-screen --permission-mode bypassPermissions', ready='│ ❯', trust gate 'Run Grok Build in a project directory'→Enter, working='Ctrl+Enter:interject', HookType=grok_hooks (grok reads drop-in ~/.grok/hooks/*.json — always trusted, registration IS the file, unlike claude/codex manual registration). Grok exec: grok -p <prompt> --output-format streaming-json (verified live: thought/text chunks + end event w/ sessionId for --resume). Generic hook script extended to parse grok payload (hookEventName/toolName camelCase keys, snake_case values) + --agent ${ARCMUX_HOOK_AGENT}. Daemon: replace agent-name conditionals (create/restore/agentStartCommand/Installer.Install) with profile.HookBacked()/HookType. New ListAgents gRPC + arcmux-cli agents + arcmux-cli exec (create exec session AutoClose + SendPrompt WaitIdle + Capture). Docs: docs/state-dir.md for external subscribers (mission-control) of ~/data/arcmux. Skill updated.
acceptance_criteria: 1) internal/llm (or equivalent) defines one LLM class per agent (codex, claude, grok) carrying both its interactive tmux profile and its exec driver; profile.DefaultProfiles derives from the class registry so adding an LLM is one struct. 2) grok works in both modes: 'arcmux-cli create --agent grok' spawns it in a tmux pane under the arcmux socket; 'arcmux-cli exec --agent grok -- <prompt>' (or grok_exec profile) runs grok -p headless and returns output. 3) Hook installation is registry-driven (no hard-coded agent name conditionals in daemon create path); hook events for any LLM land in the centralized ~/data/arcmux state (sessions/<id>.json + hook-output JSONL) readable by any subscriber; documented schema/path doc exists for external consumers (mission-control). 4) Delivery verification uses hooks judge for LLMs with hooks; grok (no hooks) degrades gracefully to heuristic. 5) Discovery: callers can list available LLM classes + transports (CLI command or HTTP). 6) make validate green (gofmt, vet, go test ./..., build); unit tests cover the class registry, grok profile, exec driver routing. 7) arcmux skill (~/agents/blin-agents/skills/arcmux) updated to document both call paths.
notes: Follow-up 0a6961b: default delivery judge is now 'auto' (hooks → typesafe → heuristic cascade) per Boyan directive — hooks always win when available; previously all real daemons defaulted to typesafe. Live-verified: default-config daemon + grok delivery → judge_source=hooks.
close_reason: Shipped: internal/profile.Class registry (codex/claude/grok × interactive tmux + headless exec) with DefaultProfiles derived from classes; grok interactive profile (ready/trust/working patterns verified live) + grok_exec driver (streaming-json parser w/ resume via sessionId); hook install + env seeding + loader wrapping now keyed by profile.HookType/HookBacked() — no agent-name conditionals; grok hooks fully auto-registered via drop-in ~/.grok/hooks/arcmux-session.json; generic hook script parses both claude and grok payload dialects and attributes via ARCMUX_HOOK_AGENT; per-session grok leader socket (session_start_args) fixes hooks running in env-less shared leader (verified: leader exits with session); ListAgents gRPC + arcmux-cli agents + arcmux-cli exec (class resolution, --keep/--session resume, --json, --timeout); docs/llm-classes.md + docs/state-dir.md subscriber contract; skill updated. Verified: make validate green; unit tests for classes/parsers/installers/script dialects; live e2e scripts/e2e/hooks-judge-live.sh grok PASS (prompt_submit+turn_end in ~/data state, prompt_ingested gated); live exec smoke returned grok output. Residual: judge_source may report heuristic when screen evidence wins the first-poll race (hooks evidence still recorded); claude/codex hook registration remains manual (arcmux-kxv/arcmux-urc).

title: Real-agent e2e: hooks judge gates a live claude/codex delivery
description: Register claude arcmux-session-hook.sh in ~/.claude/settings.json (UserPromptSubmit+Stop+PreToolUse+PostToolUse); build committed repeatable e2e (scripts/e2e/hooks-judge-live.sh + make target) that starts daemon judge=hooks, spawns real claude via arcmux-cli, sends prompt, asserts prompt_ingested judge_source=hooks + session state prompt_submit. Exposed gap: daemon drops the hook script but never registers it in claude settings, so native hook never fires.
close_reason: Claude arcmux-session-hook.sh now registered in ~/.claude/settings.json (blueprint template claude/settings.json + live; blin-agents 2edaf9d) on UserPromptSubmit/PreToolUse/PostToolUse/Stop with test -f guard. Repeatable e2e existed (scripts/e2e/hooks-judge-live.sh / make validate-e2e-hooks); ran live post-registration: PASS with prompt_ingested judge_source=hooks confidence=0.97 and session-state prompt_submit evidence. In-daemon auto-registration remains tracked as arcmux-urc.

title: Split delivery judges + add hooks-based judge
description: Move TypesafeJudge into judge_typesafe.go, add HooksJudge (judge_hooks.go) reading ~/data/arcmux/sessions/<id>.json mutated by an 'arcmux hook' CLI from claude+codex hooks. Config [delivery].judge picks one judge. Spec: docs/superpowers/specs/2026-06-03-judge-split-and-hooks-judge-design.md
notes: Path update (d16a076): hooks judge session state now reads the PROTOCOL dir ~/data/mux/sessions (was ~/data/arcmux/sessions); default judge is now 'auto' (hooks→typesafe→heuristic, 0a6961b).
close_reason: Epic complete: judges split (judge_typesafe.go/judge_hooks.go, 77d70a7), hooks judge reads protocol state ~/data/mux/sessions (d16a076), config [delivery].judge selects, default now auto-cascade hooks→typesafe→heuristic (0a6961b), live e2e green for claude+grok (judge_source=hooks 0.97).

title: Session state never transitions working->idle (tmux transport): stuck 'working' forever after agent finishes
description: After a prompt is delivered SetState(working) is called, but nothing ever transitions a tmux-backed session back to idle: the health monitor only does idle->working, working->stuck, and ->exited; the hook watcher tails Stop events into a map that no consumer reads. Observed: codex and claude both report state=working 70s+ after answering and going visually idle at their prompt. Fix: add a screen-quiescence working->idle transition to the health monitor (output unchanged for a quiescence window AND no WorkingIndicator present).
close_reason: Fixed: added screen-quiescence working->idle transition to health monitor (quiescentIdle + idleQuiescence, per-session runState). Guarded by WorkingIndicator so a busy-but-quiet agent is not falsely idled; added WorkingIndicator 'esc to interrupt' to claude profile (codex already had 'Working'). Verified live on both agents incl. a 25s silent task that correctly stayed 'working'. Unit tests added in health/monitor_test.go.

title: Claude handshake marks session 'failed': ready pattern '>' never matches v2.x prompt glyph '❯'
description: The claude profile ReadyPattern is '>' but Claude Code v2.x renders its prompt as '❯' (U+276F) and shows no bare '>' on screen, so waitForReadyPattern times out after 25s and performHandshake fails -> session state set to 'failed'. The pane is actually fully ready (Remote Control active) and SendPrompt works, so the 'failed' state is a false negative that misleads status/ready callers. Fix: use a ReadyPattern that matches a genuinely-ready Claude TUI (e.g. the persistent footer 'bypass permissions on'), not the pre-launch shell prompt glyph.
close_reason: Fixed: claude profile ReadyPattern '>' -> 'Remote Control active' (matches the --remote-control footer present in both default and HTTP launch paths; '>' never matched v2.x glyph '❯'). Verified live: handshake now reaches idle, not failed. Regression test added in profile_test.go.

# Replace per-session Claude hook scripts with one session-aware hook

## Problem
Boyan directive from Discord 1509663488170131686: arcmux currently writes ~/.claude/hooks/arcmux-s-<session>.sh per Claude session. Change it so arcmux installs/uses a single generic hook script that derives the session-specific output path from environment supplied to the Claude session (for example ARCMUX_SESSION_ID and ARCMUX_HOOK_OUTPUT_DIR). Remove/clean up existing generated session-specific hooks like arcmux-s-*.sh. Preserve arcmux's watcher/status/ready behavior.

Follow-up design directive from Discord 1509665898401890495: if a shared handoff point is needed, use a /tmp/arcmux directory where the tmux session creator writes the session-specific environment, and make the tmux loader read that environment before launching claude/codex/etc. This keeps the hook generic while making session identity/runtime paths available to the launched agent process.

## Acceptance
No new per-session Claude hook script files are created; one generic arcmux hook is installed/updated idempotently; spawned Claude sessions receive the env needed for that hook to write the correct per-session JSONL; if file-backed env handoff is needed, the session creator writes a per-session env file under /tmp/arcmux and the tmux loader loads it before launching the agent; /tmp/arcmux and env files are permissioned safely and parsed/escaped so an untrusted writable file cannot inject shell; existing arcmux-s-*.sh files are removed/ignored by migration/cleanup; tests cover install idempotency, env-derived output path, tmux-loader env loading, cleanup, and watcher/status compatibility; PR/merge documents operational cleanup.

Post-merge deployment acceptance from Discord 1509668087086059530: after the refactor merges, rebuild/restart the live `test-elonco` elonco/arcmux runtime so this profile is actually running the new generic-hook + /tmp/arcmux env-handoff behavior. Verify on the live restarted profile that no new per-session hook scripts are created, the generic hook/env path is active, and existing stale `arcmux-s-*.sh` cleanup has run or is demonstrably ignored/removed.

## Duplicate Search
Query: Replace per-session Claude hook scripts with one session-aware hook Boyan directive from Discord 1509663488170131686: arcmux currently writes ~/.claude/hooks/arcmux-s-<session>.sh per Claude session. Change it so arcmux installs/uses a single generic hook script that derives the session-specific output path from environment supplied to the Claude session (for example ARCMUX_SESSION_ID and ARCMUX_HOOK_OUTPUT_DIR). Remove/clean up existing generated session-specific hooks like arcmux-s-*.sh. Preserve arcmux's watcher/status/ready behavior. No new per-session Claude hook script files are created; one generic arcmux hook is installed/updated idempotently; spawned Claude sessions receive the env needed for that hook to write the correct per-session JSONL; existing arcmux-s-*.sh files are removed/ignored by migration/cleanup; tests cover install idempotency, env-derived output path, cleanup, and watcher/status compatibility; PR/merge documents operational cleanup.
Similar tickets: none

Security gate verified in code: envfile.go does Lstat symlink-reject + uid-ownership + 0o077 perm mask + ARCMUX_ allowlist + NUL/newline reject + POSIX single-quote escaping; loader (daemon.go agentStartCommand:582) prefixes eval $(<abs-arcmux> hook-env <id>) via os.Executable, evals arcmux's OWN quoted output, never sources raw file; hookenv.go fails safe (error->no stdout, exit 0). go test ./... all green, gofmt+vet clean. MaliciousValueRoundTripsAsLiteral + perms/symlink/disallowed-key + fail-safe tests present. Post-merge live deploy/verification PENDING (manager owns detached rebuild+restart).

title: Mute 'command finished' notifications (zero-signal noise)
description: Moved from mission-control-haw (misfiled there — mc emits no OS notifications; arcmux does). All notifications that merely say a command/tool finished are pure noise (0 signal). Suppress that class entirely while keeping meaningful ones (agent needs input, error/failure, blocked, done-with-result). Emitter lives in internal/manager/cmuxcli/notify.go; hook events (Notification/Stop) flow through cmd/arcmux. Audit the hook->notify path and drop the 'completed'/'command finished' class.
acceptance_criteria: Given an agent/tool merely finishes a command, no OS notification fires. Given a meaningful event (needs-input, error, blocked, done-with-result), a notification still fires. Verified live by triggering both a plain command-finish and a needs-input event and observing only the latter notifies.

title: Manage Codex remote-server singleton
description: arcmux should be able to start Codex remote-server by running cdx remote-server in a tmux session, stop it by killing that process, and refuse to start a duplicate when the per-computer singleton is already established.
design: HTTP /session/new keeps Claude on cld --remote-control, changes Codex to the requested interactive tmux command cdx remote-server, and guards Codex as a singleton by returning an existing active Codex remote-server session instead of creating another tmux pane.
acceptance_criteria:
- arcmux can start Codex remote-server in tmux using cdx remote-server.
- Stop kills the Codex remote-server process/session.
- Start is idempotent: if a Codex remote-server is already established/running, arcmux reports that state instead of starting another.
- Status exposes enough state for the Vox app to grey out Codex when already running.
- Go tests/build pass.
notes: cdx is not on non-interactive PATH in this shell, but tmux launch sends the command into an interactive shell where the user's cdx wrapper/alias is expected to exist.
close_reason: Implemented HTTP Codex remote-server launch via cdx remote-server, tagged remote_server/current_command state in create/list responses, guarded Codex remote-server as a live singleton, and close now kills the tmux session when available. Verified go test ./internal/daemon -run 'TestAgentStartCommand|TestIsCodexRemoteServerSnapshot' and git diff --check; go test ./... only fails unrelated internal/config TestDeliveryDefaults path expectation.

title: Optionally auto-register claude/codex hooks in agent config on daemon start
description: Today arcmux drops arcmux-session-hook.sh / arcmux-codex-hook.sh but does NOT register them in ~/.claude/settings.json or ~/.codex/hooks.json — registration is machine-local manual. Consider idempotent RMW auto-registration (preserving existing entries) so the hooks judge works on a fresh machine without manual steps. Surfaced by the real-agent e2e (scripts/e2e/hooks-judge-live.sh).
acceptance_criteria: Config [hooks] auto_register (default false, opt-in per issue title). When true, daemon Start idempotently RMW-registers: (1) the generic session hook into ~/.claude/settings.json hooks (UserPromptSubmit/PreToolUse/PostToolUse/Stop, test -f guarded command, preserving ALL existing entries + unrelated keys byte-for-byte where untouched; skip events already containing the arcmux command); (2) the codex bridge into ~/.codex/hooks.json (same RMW discipline, matching the existing live shape). Never duplicates entries on repeated starts; malformed/missing files handled (create minimal valid file only if absent); unit tests cover fresh file, existing-other-hooks preservation, idempotent re-run, and malformed-JSON refusal (non-fatal warn). make validate green.
notes: Update 2026-06-09: claude hook is now registered at the BLUEPRINT layer (blin-agents claude/settings.json, commit 2edaf9d) — live e2e passes judge_source=hooks. This issue remains open only for the in-daemon auto-registration variant (useful on machines not managed by the blueprint). State dir is now ~/data/mux/sessions (d16a076).
close_reason: Added opt-in [hooks].auto_register (default false) and daemon-start RMW registration for Claude settings.json and Codex hooks.json; tests cover fresh files, existing-hook/key preservation, idempotent reruns, malformed JSON refusal, /Users/blin duplicate detection, and daemon opt-in wiring; make validate overall: pass.
